name: Vault — Business Banking
description: Fintech prototype stress-testing LUMEN on dense financial UI: balance hero, transactions ledger with 50+ rows, multi-step send-money flow with typed-amount confirmation, cards, team, accounts. Introduces Amount, AmountConfirmDialog, and LineChart/SparkLine primitives.
openQuestions: Should Amount ship with a built-in delta/trend indicator prop or stay pure?
AmountConfirmDialog: promote to real DS after how many prototypes use it?
Do the chart wrappers (LineChart/SparkLine) deserve a dedicated @lumen/charts pack, or ship to core?
Stepper component shows 'Step NaN of 4' aria label — needs a look at required props

prompts.text: Build a fintech prototype (Vault — business banking) to stress-test LUMEN on dense financial UI. Introduces Amount component, AmountConfirmDialog with typed-amount verification, Recharts-based LineChart/SparkLine wrappers, and a tabular-nums typography utility. Dense, precise, trustworthy visual direction. Lumen Dark default theme.

context.summary: Second LUMEN stress test — deliberately picked a domain opposite the marketing site: dense numeric UI, real-time data, trust signals, destructive confirmations, tabular alignment. Tests whether 'one system, every product' holds across radically different density and tone.
context.notes: Explicitly built with Recharts as a wrapped primitive — charts are theme-aware via LUMEN tokens. Amount component shipped to real DS; AmountConfirmDialog kept prototype-local until second use validates the API.

name: LUMEN Marketing Site
description: Marketing site for the LUMEN Design System — hero + live 7-theme switcher, component wall, accessibility proof, and component gallery. Dogfoods LUMEN: every pixel is a design system component.
openQuestions: Should the footer link to a real GitHub repo once LUMEN is published?
Does the Themes page need a 'Bring your own theme' CTA flow?
Should we add a changelog or release notes page in v2?

prompts.text: Build a LUMEN marketing site with Landing, Themes, Components, and Get Started pages. Editorial/bold direction. Live theme switcher across all 7 showcase themes. Dogfoods the design system — every element must be a LUMEN component or token.

context.summary: First non-Lumen stress test of LUMEN as a general design system. Proves the theme architecture by making it the hero of the site.
context.notes: Built to pressure-test LUMEN in a marketing context (not enterprise/admin). Success criteria: positioning clear in 30s, theme switcher earns an 'oh', 100% DS dogfooding, feels like a product not a doc site.
